title: DSL LSP server
description: Lightweight Language Server Protocol implementation for .dsl files. Reads the node reference JSON (from DSL-6) at startup to know all available nodes and their ports. Provides: completions for node IDs after '= ', port name completions after '(' and ',', mode enum completions for string-typed ports with ModeConstraints, hover info showing port types and defaults, diagnostics from GraphValidator (from DSL-7). The schema is finite (45+ nodes with typed ports) so the LSP is a thin wrapper over the reference data, not a full compiler.
definition-of-done: 1. LSP server starts and connects to Cursor/VSCode. 2. .dsl files get syntax diagnostics (unknown nodes, wrong ports, type mismatches). 3. Typing '= ' after a variable name offers node ID completions. 4. Typing a port name inside () offers available port names for that node. 5. Hover over a node ID shows its inputs/outputs/description. 6. Mode-constrained string ports (e.g., operation=) offer enum completions (ADD, SUBTRACT, etc.).
testing-plan: 1. Open a .dsl file in Cursor, verify completions appear for node IDs. 2. Verify port name completions inside parentheses. 3. Introduce an error (wrong port name), verify diagnostic appears. 4. Hover over a node, verify tooltip shows port info.
todos: Choose LSP implementation language (Python with pygls, or TypeScript)
Load node reference JSON at startup
Implement textDocument/completion for node IDs and port names
Add .dsl file association to the extension
unknowns: Whether to implement in Python (pygls — can reuse existing CLI infrastructure) or TypeScript (native VSCode extension pattern)
Whether to call dsl-validate as a subprocess or reimplement validation in the LSP language
How to handle incremental parsing for real-time diagnostics without lag
